title: Changelog
subtitle: Implemented product updates and a practical guide to the features currently available in DocBook.

releases.title: AI writing and circle select
releases.summary: DocBook now includes an in-app AI workspace with streaming responses, direct note writing, and circle-to-select editing.
changes: Added DocBook AI chat mode for note-aware conversations inside the editor.
Added append mode so AI can write rich HTML directly into the active note.
Added circle-to-edit overlay so you can draw around text to select it before continuing your AI workflow.
Improved the AI writing flow with in-editor streaming and stop controls.
how_to_use: Press Ctrl+D to open the AI panel.
Use Chat Mode to ask for suggestions, rewrites, summaries, or action items based on the active note.
Switch to Append Mode when you want AI to write formatted content directly into the note.
Use Circle in Append Mode, draw around text, and continue editing with that selection active.

releases.title: Customization, reactions, and workspace polish
releases.summary: The editor now supports customizable people, folder references, emoji reactions, onboarding, install prompts, and richer workspace navigation.
changes: Added customization panel for people, folders, and emoji reaction sets.
Added create-on-the-fly @mentions and #folder references from the editor suggestion menus.
Added sidebar emoji reactions for notes using a selectable emoji set.
Added welcome intro modal for first-run onboarding.
Added install prompt handling so the app can be installed as a PWA from supported browsers.
Added feedback and plan screens in the workspace flow.
how_to_use: Type @ in the editor to search people or create a new person inline.
Type # in the editor to search folders or create a new folder reference inline.
Open Customization from the mention or folder picker to manage saved people, folders, and emojis.
Hover a note in the sidebar and click an emoji to react to it.
Use Download App from the sidebar when the browser exposes an install prompt.

releases.title: Connections and changelog
releases.summary: DocBook now supports searchable note-to-note connections and an in-app changelog view.
changes: Added searchable note connections between any two notes.
Added backlinks so you can see which notes point to the current one.
Added a docked right-side connections tray inside the editor.
Added a sidebar changelog entry with an in-app changelog panel.
how_to_use: Open any note and use the connections tray on the right edge.
Hover or click the tray to expand it.
Click the search button inside the tray to find another note.
Select a note from the results to connect it.
Use the left sidebar Changelog item to open this changelog view.

releases.title: Writing tools and editor cleanup
releases.summary: The main writing surface was refined with more commands, cleaner controls, and better organization.
changes: Improved editor toolbar spacing and reduced header clutter.
Added slash commands for notes, dates, and todo insertion.
Added /map insertion for quick location references.
Added inline table insertion and richer block formatting controls.
Added highlight and clear actions for selections and rich chips.
Added mention and folder reference chips inside notes.
Improved image handling and selection panel flow.
how_to_use: Type /note to create or open a sticky note from the editor.
Type /date to insert formatted dates like today, tomorrow, or custom day phrases.
Type /todo to insert todo rows.
Type /map and continue with a place name to insert a location line.
Type @ to insert a person mention chip.
Type # to insert a folder/path reference chip.
Click the image/selection button when images are attached to inspect them.

releases.title: Core DocBook system
releases.summary: The app foundation was set up for notes, sticky notes, backup, local storage, and sync settings.
changes: Added rich note editing with local IndexedDB persistence.
Added floating sticky notes that can move across notes.
Added note color and note text scaling controls.
Added soft delete with restore flow for removed notes.
Added import/export backup actions in the sidebar.
Added cloud sync settings with PIN-based sync.
how_to_use: Create notes from the sidebar plus button.
Change note color from the color dots in the editor header.
Use the text size controls to scale note content.
Create sticky notes from the toolbar or /note command.
Drag sticky notes across the board and drop them onto a note in the sidebar to move them.
Use Export Backup or Import Backup from the lower-left sidebar actions.
Open Cloud Sync from the lower-left sidebar to save a PIN, push notes, pull notes, or enable auto-sync.

features.name: Notes
features.description: Create and organize long-form notes with color, title, and rich formatting.
how_to_use: Use the sidebar plus button to create a new note.
Click any note in the sidebar to open it.
Edit the title from the top-left title field.
Use the color dots and size buttons in the editor header to personalize the note.

features.name: Sticky notes
features.description: Keep quick floating ideas near your main note.
how_to_use: Click the sticky note button in the editor header or type /note.
Drag sticky notes around the page to reposition them.
Drop a sticky note on another note in the sidebar to move it to that note.
Use the eye toggle in the editor header to hide or show the sticky note board.

features.name: Connections
features.description: Connect notes together like a knowledge graph without leaving the note view.
how_to_use: Open the right-side connections tray.
Use the tray search to find another note.
Click a result to connect it.
Click a connected note to jump there, or remove it with the close icon.

features.name: DocBook AI
features.description: Ask note-aware questions or have AI stream formatted content directly into the active note.
how_to_use: Press Ctrl+D to open the AI panel.
Use Chat Mode for suggestions, summaries, edits, and brainstorming.
Use Append Mode to let AI write structured HTML content into the active note.
Stop generation with the Stop button or the floating cancel button while AI is writing.

features.name: Circle to edit
features.description: Select text by drawing around it, then continue editing with the AI workflow.
how_to_use: Open the AI panel and switch to Append Mode.
Click Circle and draw around the text you want selected.
After the selection is captured, continue your AI editing flow on that content.

features.name: Keyboard shortcuts
features.description: Use quick shortcuts to navigate core parts of the workspace faster.
how_to_use: Press Ctrl+K or Cmd+K to open command search.
Press Ctrl+D to open DocBook AI.
Press Ctrl+H to open Cloud Sync settings.
Press Ctrl+S to save the current note manually.

features.name: Command search
features.description: Jump to notes quickly with the global search overlay.
how_to_use: Press Ctrl+K or Cmd+K.
Type part of a note title or content.
Use arrow keys to move through results and Enter to open one.

features.name: Slash commands
features.description: Insert structured content quickly while writing.
how_to_use: Type /date to insert a date.
Type /todo to insert todo rows.
Type /note to open or create sticky notes.
Type /map to insert a location prompt.

features.name: Rich editor tools
features.description: Format selections with highlights, links, headings, lists, tables, images, and interactive todos.
how_to_use: Select text to open the floating formatting controls.
Use the toolbar to highlight, clear styling, add links, change text format, or insert tables.
Click a todo checkbox in the editor to toggle it between complete and incomplete.

features.name: Mentions and folders
features.description: Reference people and folders directly inside note content.
how_to_use: Type @ to search people.
Type # to search folder references.
Use arrow keys and Enter to insert the selected chip.
If there is no match, create a new person or folder directly from the suggestion menu.

features.name: Images and selection panel
features.description: Attach images to notes and inspect the current selection or image list.
how_to_use: Paste an image with Ctrl+V or drag an image into the editor.
Attach images to a selected range when available.
Use the image/selection button in the editor header to open the selection panel.

features.name: Cloud sync
features.description: Sync note text across devices with a PIN-based cloud flow.
how_to_use: Open Cloud Sync from the bottom-left sidebar.
Create or enter your PIN.
Use Sync to Cloud or Pull from Cloud.
Enable auto-sync and choose an interval if needed.

features.name: Backups
features.description: Export and import note backups locally.
how_to_use: Use Export Backup from the bottom-left sidebar to download notes as JSON.
Use Import Backup to merge notes from a saved JSON backup.

features.name: Customization
features.description: Customize people, folders, and emoji reactions used in the editor and sidebar.
how_to_use: Open the customization panel from the mention or folder suggestion menu.
Add or edit people for @mentions.
Add or edit folders for #references.
Choose which emoji reactions are available in the sidebar.

features.name: Feedback
features.description: Send product feedback directly from inside the app.
how_to_use: Open Feedback from the sidebar.
Enter your message in the feedback panel.
Submit it from the modal.

features.name: Install and onboarding
features.description: Start with a guided intro and install the app for a more native workspace experience.
how_to_use: Use the welcome screen on first open to enter the workspace.
Choose Download App from the sidebar when installation is available in your browser.
